message:
perf(extensions): enum-based session hostcall dispatch replacing string matching

Continue the allocation-free dispatch migration by applying the same
pattern to session hostcalls that was used for events hostcalls.

Add SessionHostcallOp enum with all 14 session operations (AppendMessage,
AppendEntry, GetState, GetMessages, GetEntries, GetBranch, GetFile,
GetName, SetName, SetModel, GetModel, SetThinkingLevel, GetThinkingLevel,
SetLabel) and parse_session_hostcall_op() using with_folded_ascii_alnum_token
byte-slice matching.

Convert dispatch_hostcall_session_ref() from a chain of
token_eq_ascii_folded() if-else branches and CommonHostcallOpcode matching
to a single match on SessionHostcallOp, with the unknown-op error moved
to a single early-return guard before the match. This eliminates the
per-call string normalization and opcode parsing overhead on the session
dispatch path.

Consolidate ctx_cache invalidation into a declarative matches!() guard
at the top of the function (AppendMessage, AppendEntry, SetName, SetModel,
SetThinkingLevel, SetLabel) instead of setting a mutable flag inside
individual branches.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
